name: Pan-African flag (UNIA / Afro-American flag)

colors.symbolism: the blood that unites all people of African ancestry, shed for liberation
Black people whose existence as a nation, though not a nation-state, is affirmed by the existence of the flag
the abundant natural wealth of Africa

description: A horizontal triband of three equal stripes — red on top, black in the middle and green at the bottom.
history: Adopted on 13 August 1920 by the Universal Negro Improvement Association and African Communities League (UNIA-ACL) at its convention in New York City, in response to the racist 1900 coon-song 'Every Race Has a Flag but the Coon'. Designed under the leadership of Marcus Garvey, it has been used internationally as the flag of Pan-Africanism, the African diaspora and Black liberation movements; several African post-independence flags adopted the same red-black-green palette in homage.
